550:
1. Welcome to Arena!
2. Attack other players to earn rating points.
3. In Arena battles, gold for killed units is tripled.
4. The number of attacks is limited to 20 attempts per day.
5. Rating points increase when you win and decrease when you lose.
6. Other players may attack you. Attack back to regain your lost rating – tap the Revenge button in Duels Archive.
7. Revenge returns half of lost points.
8. Get a reward at the end of the season: for the maximum league reached and rating points above 4000.
9. The season lasts two weeks.
10. Any unfair play and using cheats are prohibited.

Welcome to Expeditions Mode! 
<sprite color=#882D43 name="text_bullet"> Complete expeditions to earn gold and iron. 
<sprite color=#882D43 name="text_bullet"> Get units of a certain faction for completing expeditions that belong to that faction. 
<sprite color=#882D43 name="text_bullet"> The number of expeditions per day depends on the mode's global level. 
<sprite color=#882D43 name="text_bullet"> Mythical Heroes complete expeditions 50% faster, Legendary heroes – 40% faster, and Epic heroes – 25% faster. 
<sprite color=#882D43 name="text_bullet"> Expeditions refresh once a day. 
<sprite color=#882D43 name="text_bullet"> Spend iron in the Expedition Shop on equipment to strengthen your character and army.

1. Welcome to Glorious Hunt mode! 
2. The season lasts 3 days. 
3. Each day you'll have 3 tries to participate in the Glorious Hunt. 
4. Top players will be formed according to the total damage dealt to the Boss for the whole season. 
5. After each battle, you'll get coins depending on the damage dealt. 
6. Boss has immunity to some troop and hero skills, and its power increases every 30 sec of battle. 
7. Demo battles don't waste a boss fight attempt, but also don't earn gold.

4. Each chapter has unique free and premium rewards.

986:
1. Welcome to the Temple of Fusion!
2. Here you can get a troop with a higher rarity by fusing three troops of the same rarity.
3. There are 3 types of fusion. Each will produce troops of a certain rarity. By fusing simple troops, you'll get rare ones, epic troops come from rare troops, and legendary squads from epic troops.
4. The level of the fused troops determines the probability of the level of the new troop created by fusion.
5. After fusing troops in the Temple of Fusion, the troops you fused will disappear forever.

1185:
1. Welcome to Clans.
2. Clans are an integral part of PVP and the lifeblood of your in-game experience.
3. Each clan has a chat where you can talk to other players, share your success, and ask for help.
4. The clan hierarchy consists of the Leader, Advisor, and Members. There can only be one Leader in a clan.
5. Leaders can change clan settings, upgrade, demote, and remove any player from their clan.
6. Advisors can kick out players from the clan who are below them in rank.
7. Leaders can delegate their responsibilities, after which they become Advisors.

3. Battle floors have both debuffs and buffs that affect the player's army. Choose your units and deployment carefully on each new floor.

5. Floors are unlocked using keys.
6. Keys are restored once per hour until the limit is reached.

1664:
<line-indent=40>Participation in the tournament is available after reaching campaign Level 500.
Each tournament is dedicated to a specific faction. Players can only fight with heroes and units in the current tournament faction.
The number of troops available to place in a formation has been reduced to 25, and the number of heroes has been expanded to 5.
After the tournament begins, players can set up a defensive formation to repel enemy attacks.
The tournament repeats every 2 weeks.
The tournament lasts 4 days, each day with its own special features.
Rewards for winners are sent by email at the end of each tournament day.
1666: During the qualifying day, players test their strength against tournament monsters. The more damage you deal, the higher your place on the Leaderboard. The Top 2.000 players from the qualifying day advance. Leaderboard takes into account the best result from a single battle, but you can battle as many times as you want to improve your result.
1668: Day 1: The first day of the main stage of the tournament, where players compete against armies of other players in their group. You need to be in the Top 4 of your group by the end of the tournament day to advance. The Top 400 players of the day will advance to the next stage. Day 2: The second day of the main stage of the tournament. You need to finish at the top of your group by the end of the tournament day to advance. The Top 20 players of the day will advance to the next stage. Day 3: The final day of the tournament, where the strongest players from the previous stages fight for the championship title. The outcome of each fight depends on the defensive and offensive battles. Victory: Based on the results of 2 battles, you have either more living units left than the enemy, or you managed to win both battles. Winning earns you +3 points. Draw: You and the enemy have an equal number of living units left after 2 battles. For a draw, you get +1 point. Defeat: At the end of 2 battles, you have fewer living units left than the enemy, or you failed to win both battles. You don't earn points for losing, but you don't lose them either. Disqualification: The player leaves the game during the attack battle, or doesn't attack the enemy at all. Points aren't lost for disqualification, but they aren't earned either.

1763:
The event is all about strengthening your army by summoning troops. For each summoned troop, you earn event points based on the troop's rarity and level. The higher the level and rarity, the more points!
 Event points are earned for troops received in the following cases:
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Hired for gold and in Campaign chests
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Troops bought in the Shop
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Troops received in the Battle Pass

 Points are also awarded for troops received in the following modes:
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Wheel of Fortune
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Expeditions
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Glory Road
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Headhunt
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Library
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Conquests
 <sprite name="text_bullet" color=#84253D> Clan Chests

 As you earn points, you move up the event scale and receive various rewards.
 At the end of the event, players receive a reward based on their place in the Group and Global Leaderboard.

1816:
1. Welcome to Arena! 
2. Attack other players to earn rating points. 
3. Gold for defeated units doubles in Arena Battles. 
4. The number of attacks is limited to 20 attempts per day. 
5. Rating points increase when you win and decrease when you lose. 
6. Other players may attack you. Attack back to regain your lost rating – tap the Revenge button in Duels Archive. 
7. Get a reward at the end of the season: for the maximum league reached and rating points above 4000. 
8. The season lasts two weeks. 
9. Any unfair play and using cheats are prohibited.

1924:
1. Clan Hunts are held weekly Monday to Wednesday.
2. Two clans participate in each hunt and compete for total damage dealt to the boss. The clan that deals the most damage wins.
3. The Hunt Leaderboard includes players from both clans.
4. Players earn personal points for damage dealt to the boss, and points are converted into clan currency at the end of the event.
5. Personal points are only awarded if the player is a member of the clan participating in the hunt.
6. Players who join a clan after the start of a hunt are not eligible to participate in it.
7. Clan Rating is updated after the hunt based on outcome: victory, defeat, or draw.
8. Following the hunt, all participants are sent a message with the clan currency they earned.
9. The amount of clan currency has been temporarily increased until new clan activities are introduced.

1. Items in the Clan Shop are sold for clan coins.
2. Coins are awarded to players for participating in Clan Clashes, and their tier depends on the clan rank:
<sprite name="text_bullet" color=#972945> From rank 1 to 4, Tier 1 coin is awarded <sprite name="icon_clan_currency_1"> 
<sprite name="text_bullet" color=#972945> From rank 5 to 8, Tier 2 coins are awarded <sprite name="icon_clan_currency_2"> 
<sprite name="text_bullet" color=#972945> After rank 9, Tier 3 coins are awarded <sprite name="icon_clan_currency_3"> 
3. Clan members can purchase lower-ranked items using higher-tier coins at a 1 to 1 ratio.
4. The shop is updated daily.
1967: Remus powers up the regular attacks of allied troops to deal more damage to enemy tanks and bosses. The effect continues after the hero's death.
1970: Atreya inspires allied units, granting their attacks a chance to deal increased damage to enemy units. The effect remains active even after the hero's death.
1976: Calira summons pillars of light that periodically restore health to all allied units on the battlefield.
1980: When the squad takes damage from a normal attack, Calira's light fully restores the squad's health over time. The effect continues after the hero's death.
1983: Harkon enchants allied units so they only take a portion of incoming damage. The remaining damage is taken gradually once per second for 5 seconds. The effect continues after the hero's death but does not work in boss battles.

1997:
1. The event lasts 7 days.
2. Players complete quests and earn points to level up the event's Battle Pass.
3. Battle Pass rewards include tickets you can spend on special roulette spins to win valuable prizes.
4. Every 100 spins is a guaranteed event hero.
5. When the event ends, all unused tickets will be canceled.
6. Good luck!

2081:
<line-indent=5%>In this update, we reworked the hero collection bonus system.</line-indent>
<line-indent=5%>Collections no longer provide upgrades. Instead, now there's a  bonus for each hero in the game.</line-indent>
<line-indent=5%>The bonus is cumulative for all of a player's available heroes.</line-indent>
<line-indent=5%>The higher the hero's level and rarity, the bigger the bonus.</line-indent>
<line-indent=5%>Bonus affects the HP, base attack, and abilities of all of the player's combat units.</line-indent>
2086: Made from thin but durable tanned leather.
2088: Simple protection for novice commanders. Lightweight and comfortable, but can't compare to metal in combat.
2090: Crafted from the chitinous plates of a giant spider and laced with magical threads, this lightweight but durable armor doesn't restrict movement.
2092: Forged from the enchanted scales of a fallen dragon, this armor is imbued with impenetrable durability and magical power.
2094: Forged from enchanted sea monster plates and entwined with tentacles to grant its wearer the wrath of the ocean.
2096: The armor of renowned gladiators tempered in bloody battle. Grants the wearer lightning-fast speed and the fortitude to withstand crushing blows.

2240:
1. Clan Clashes run weekly from Thursday to Sunday.
2. Day 1 – Preparation: Set and save your formation (it will be locked for the event).
3. Days 2-4 – Battles: Fight enemy clan players up to 3 times/day.
4. Battles are auto-fought (max 5 minutes).
5. Points scale with your army's power, enemy strength, and units defeated.
6. The clan with the most points wins.
7. Players who joined the clan while the event is active cannot participate in battles.
8. When the event is over, earned points will be converted to clan currency.
9. The amount of clan currency has been temporarily increased until new clan activities are introduced.
